paper.title: 8.9-hr Rotation in the Partly Burnt Runaway Stellar Remnant LP 40-365 (GD 492)

review.summary: This ApJ Letter reports 8.914-hr photometric variability in LP 40-365 detected from TESS, HST/STIS, and WISE light curves. No LaTeX tables are present in the source. Two external resources identified: the stistools Python package used to process HST/STIS time-tagged photon data, and the IRSA archive queried for AllWISE/NEOWISE photometry. The paper acknowledges TESS, HST, and WISE data but provides no concrete data-access URLs beyond these two resources.

external_resources.description: Python package from STScI for interacting with HST/STIS data, used to bin time-tagged photon data into 3-min intervals and extract an ultraviolet light curve.

source_refs.context: We interacted with these data using the python package \texttt{stistools}\footnote{\href{https://stistools.readthedocs.io/}{https://stistools.readthedocs.io}} available from the Space Telescope Science Institute.

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.2 (HST Observations): the URL is given explicitly in the \href command linking to https://stistools.readthedocs.io/.

external_resources.description: NASA/IPAC Infrared Science Archive queried via AllWISE Multiepoch Photometry Table and NEOWISE-R Single Exposure Source Table to obtain W1 and W2 band photometry for LP 40-365 from 2010–2019.

source_refs.context: We queried the AllWISE Multiepoch Photometry Table and NEOWISE-R Single Exposure Source Table\footnote{Available at {\href{https://irsa.ipac.caltech.edu}{irsa.ipac.caltech.edu}}}, obtaining photometry in the \textit{W1} and \textit{W2} bands, taken during the decade 2010--2019.

external_resources.evidence: Footnote in Section 2.3 (WISE Observations): the URL is given explicitly as irsa.ipac.caltech.edu.
